focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Nora begins as a principled gatekeeper, resisting compromises in children's television, but is gradually thrust into escalating moral crises that force her from rigid idealism toward active, often violent, engagement. By the end, she has shed her detachment, embracing both emotional intimacy and lethal agency to protect Sheldon and dismantle corruption, yet remains poised on the edge of unresolved consequences, her integrity tested by the very brutality she now wields.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Sheldon Mopes begins as an idealistic performer seeking recognition, only to be publicly humiliated and stripped of agency by the very system he hoped to influence, but he gradually transforms into a defiant moral force, rejecting corruption and reclaiming his integrity through ethical stands—yet his hard-won redemption is shadowed by unresolved guilt, emotional isolation, and the looming cost of principled resistance in a world where power and violence refuse to relinquish control.
